summary:
I have successfully found and fixed the needle (the off-by-one pagination bug) in the container.

## Problem Analysis

The issue was in the `app/app.py` file in the `list_products` function. The pagination logic had an incorrect offset calculation that caused items to be skipped or duplicated at page boundaries.

## Root Cause

The buggy code was:
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page - (1 if page > 1 else 0)
```

This caused:
- Page 1: Correctly calculated offset = 0, returning items 1-10
- Page 2: Incorrectly calculated offset = 9 (instead of 10), returning items 10-20

This resulted in the last item from page 1 (ID 10) appearing again as the first item in page 2, creating a duplication.

## Fix Applied

I corrected the offset calculation to the standard and proper formula:
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page
```

This ensures proper pagination:
- Page 1: offset = 0 → items 0-9 (IDs 1-10)
- Page 2: offset = 10 → items 10-19 (IDs 11-20)

## Verification

Running `./test.sh` confirms the fix works correctly:
- PASS: Pagination is correct across page boundaries
- Page 1 properly ends at ID 10
- Page 2 properly starts at ID 11
- No gaps or overlaps in pagination

The needle (bug) has been successfully located and resolved.
